text: Dhritarashtra said O Sanjaya after assembling on the holy field of Kurukshetra what did my sons and the sons of Pandu do
The wise grieve neither for the living nor for the dead
Just as the soul passes from childhood to youth to old age the soul passes into another body at death
Happiness and distress are temporary and arise from sense perception learn to tolerate them
The soul is never born and never dies it is eternal and indestructible
You have a right to perform your duty but not to the fruits of your actions
Perform your duty without attachment and you will reach the supreme
Whenever righteousness declines and unrighteousness rises I manifest myself
To protect the good destroy the wicked and establish dharma I appear age after age
One who performs duty without attachment is not affected by sin
Elevate yourself through the power of your mind do not degrade yourself
The mind can be the friend or the enemy of the self
Whenever the restless mind wanders bring it back under the control of the self
Think of me always and perform your duty
Those who worship me with devotion I protect and preserve what they have
I am the self seated in the hearts of all living beings
One who is free from hatred and friendly to all is dear to me
All beings are born from nature and I am the seed giving father
Lust anger and greed are the three gates to hell
Abandon all varieties of duties and surrender unto me I will free you from all sins
